human_verification.notes: Verified issue #1010 scope, NAICS 561 industry specificity, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure workflow coverage, source-class coverage, scenario-to-ability mapping, refusal triggers, research contract coverage, and specification-based runtime boundary.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit administrative service metric definitions, source lineage, refresh and confidence handling, caveats, and refusal behavior.
Required abilities across shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry profiles are fully covered and mapped.

Scenarios include ambiguous metrics, incomplete and stale data, conflicting systems, unsupported claims, client disclosure risk, industry terminology, missing safety/authority facts, adjacent-lane routing, overgeneralization refusal, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.

specialist_owned_evidence: NAICS 561 administrative support and business-services scope materially shapes metrics, artifacts, and refusal behavior.
Service analytics outputs require explicit metric definitions, denominators, source hierarchy, refresh timestamps, cadence, audience, caveats, confidence notes, and action thresholds.
Stale, incomplete, or conflicting service desk, workflow, document, audit, BI, client scorecard, retention, and access records trigger reconciliation or escalation before publication.

Administrative analytics does not become authority over operations approvals, staffing, SLA exceptions, client credits, contract changes, legal/privacy interpretation, records disposition, safety/security incidents, or final disclosure signoff.
Runtime remains specification-based, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after converting tenant variation to explicit retrieval, refusal, and handoff requirements.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment with clear promotion criteria to retained-implementation execution.
